title: Juna Mahal, Dungarpur
tagline: Juna Mahal, Dungarpur — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Juna Mahal, also known as the Old Palace or Garh Palace, is a palace complex situated in the city of Dungarpur in the Indian state of Rajasthan.
overview.description: Juna Mahal, also known as the Old Palace or Garh Palace, is a palace complex situated in the city of Dungarpur in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It is situated at the foothills of the Dhanmata Hill. The property is still owned by the former royal family of Dungarpur.

topPlaces.name: Adinath Temple, Dungarpur
topPlaces.description: Adinath Bhagwan Temple is a Jain Temple in Manek Chowk, Dungarpur, Rajasthan, India. It was built by Seth Sanvaldas Davda in 1469 CE (1526 VS).

topPlaces.name: Dungarpur state
topPlaces.description: Dungarpur State was a kingdom and later princely state during the British Raj. Its capital was the city of Dungarpur in the southernmost area of present-day Rajasthan State in India.

topPlaces.name: Dungarpur Mews
topPlaces.description: The Dungarpur Mews is an automobile museum in Dungarpur, Rajasthan, India.

topPlaces.name: Gaib Sagar Lake
topPlaces.description: Gaib Sagar Lake, also known as Gap Sagar Lake, is an artificial lake in present-day Dungarpur, Rajasthan, India. It was built by Maharawal Gopinath (Gaipa Rawal) of Dungarpur State in 1428.

topPlaces.name: Udai Bilas Palace
topPlaces.description: Udai Bilas Palace in Dungarpur, Rajasthan is the principal residence of the former royal family of Dungarpur and operates as a heritage hotel. A part of the palace is a museum.

topPlaces.name: Dungarpur, Rajasthan
topPlaces.description: Dungarpur is a city and a Municipality in the southernmost district in Rajasthan, India. Dungarpur is the headquarter of Dungarpur District.

items.note: Adinath Bhagwan Temple is a Jain Temple in Manek Chowk, Dungarpur, Rajasthan, India. It was built by Seth Sanvaldas Davda in 1469 CE (1526 VS).
Dungarpur State was a kingdom and later princely state during the British Raj. Its capital was the city of Dungarpur in the southernmost area of present-day Rajasthan State in India.
The Dungarpur Mews is an automobile museum in Dungarpur, Rajasthan, India.
Gaib Sagar Lake, also known as Gap Sagar Lake, is an artificial lake in present-day Dungarpur, Rajasthan, India. It was built by Maharawal Gopinath (Gaipa Rawal) of Dungarpur State in 1428.
Udai Bilas Palace in Dungarpur, Rajasthan is the principal residence of the former royal family of Dungarpur and operates as a heritage hotel. A part of the palace is a museum.
Dungarpur is a city and a Municipality in the southernmost district in Rajasthan, India. Dungarpur is the headquarter of Dungarpur District.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Juna Mahal, Dungarpur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Juna Mahal, Dungarpur is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Juna Mahal, Dungarpur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (90 km) and the nearest railway station is Udaipur City (84 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Juna Mahal, Dungarpur?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Juna Mahal, Dungarpur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Juna Mahal, Dungarpur?
faq.a: Stays in Juna Mahal, Dungarpur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Juna Mahal, Dungarpur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adinath Temple, Dungarpur, Dungarpur state, Dungarpur Mews, Gaib Sagar Lake, Udai Bilas Palace.
